subject: English Language Arts
topic: Novel Study: Long Way Down by Jason Reynolds

classroom_overview.title: Long Way Down Launch Organizer
classroom_overview.subtitle: Structure, pressure, and questions
classroom_overview.prompt: As you read, record one structure pattern, one pressure Will faces, and one question you want to keep tracking. Use page references or short evidence notes, not copied passages.
success_criteria: I noticed structure, repetition, or pattern.
I connected a moment to Will's pressure or conflict.
I wrote a question that matters for the next section.

reading_response.title: Long Way Down Reading Response
reading_response.subtitle: Structure, tension, and choice
reading_response.learning_target: I can explain how structure and voice shape Will's conflict.
reading_response.directions: Choose one moment from the assigned section that changes how you understand Will. Use a page reference or brief evidence note.
reading_response.prompt: How does the elevator structure affect what we learn about Will's thoughts, memories, or choices?

vocabulary.definition: a novel told through poems or poetic sections
a struggle happening inside a character
how a text is organized and how that organization shapes meaning

success_criteria: I named a specific moment or section.
I used a page reference or brief evidence note.
I explained how structure affects meaning.
I connected the moment to Will's conflict or choice.

theme_cer.title: Long Way Down CER Organizer
theme_cer.subtitle: Build a theme claim with evidence
theme_cer.question: What is the novel suggesting about rules, grief, revenge, or choice?
theme_cer.learning_target: I can build a theme claim and support it with evidence.
theme_cer.directions: Choose one theme idea. Make a claim, point to a specific moment, and explain how that moment supports your thinking.
sentence_starters: One moment that supports this is...
The reader is invited to question...
success_criteria: My claim is about a theme, not just plot.
My evidence points to a specific moment.

floor_encounter_organizer.title: Elevator Encounter Tracker
floor_encounter_organizer.subtitle: What changes after this encounter?
floor_encounter_organizer.prompt: For the assigned encounter, record what is revealed and how it changes the pressure on Will.
success_criteria: I identified what the encounter reveals.
I explained how the encounter changes tension or uncertainty.
I connected the encounter to Will's choice.

exit_reflection.title: Long Way Down Exit Reflection
exit_reflection.subtitle: Question, detail, next section
exit_reflection.learning_target: I can reflect on how today's reading changed my thinking.
exit_reflection.prompt: What question are you carrying into the next section? Which detail created that question?
success_criteria: I named the detail or structure that created it.
I explained why the question matters.
